figures.caption: Figure 3: Visual centroids mapping the continuous bouncing ball environment into an interpretable, discrete stop-motion dictionary without heuristic labels.
Figure 7: Grounded topological organization of abstract sequence data. The network autonomously clusters non-spatial concepts by causal grammatical category.

Figure 1: The GNWM Architecture. Raw inputs are processed by the Retinotopic Encoder into a spatial grid. A fixed radial filter applies Topological Smearing. The Action-Conditioned Spatial Predictor then estimates the future latent state, enforcing a strict JEPA paradigm.
Figure 4: Latent variance over a 100-step horizon. Grid snapping prevents the mean-prediction blur common in continuous architectures.

Figure 5: Agent Imagination Tree (Branching Futures). Starting from an initial state (black star), the Action-Conditioned Spatial Predictor unrolls deterministic, orthogonal branches across the latent grid for all four available actions. The network organically maps the exact empirical visitation distribution without hallucinating invalid transitions or dead nodes.
Figure 2: The Thermodynamic Gradient Flow. The batch mean is evaluated against the uniform constant (c) strictly on the target state, naturally balancing expansion and contraction forces

Figure 8: The GNWM For topological routing (TSP problem). Good solutions can be found by restarting algorithm with different random initial conditions but we picked this particular solution to show that Elastic Maps, even with end-to-end differentiable deep learning pipeline, are approximate algorithms as shown by the arrow. GitHub repository containing simulation scripts can be found here.
Figure 6: Dual-channel separation of a multi-entity environment. The GNWM autonomously allocates independent semantic maps to individual objects.
